column1: # enumeration of what the product AI may NEVER touch without a human apply-token.
# Non-comment lines are DATA. Comment lines start with '#'.
#   path_prefix        : a changed file matches this row iff its path starts with path_prefix
#   removal_authority  : out-of-band-only      -> NEVER editable by AI (D11 Q4 hard line);
#                                             in DECISIONS.md (D11 Q5) AND human editing.
#                        operator-ruling-required -> AI may edit UNDER the human apply-token,
#                                             but removal additionally requires a DECISIONS.md
#   why               : why this path is in the registry (load-bearing red line).
#   source            : REAL file:line citation on the integration tree (every row verified
#   status            : live | placeholder (placeholder = referenced machinery unbuilt here).
# §0.4 central acceptance property: the registry + gate code are CORE — unmodifiable by the
# pipeline they govern. Item 73 (the Gate-Root invariant) is recorded by this registry's own
# inclusion (the governance-self self-row below, blueprint §3.3(8)) and by the monotonicity
# guard (scripts/red-line-monotonicity.sh). This file is itself in the registry.
# Meta-law (item 73 clause, recorded here per blueprint §2.3(5)): no `pub` type / fn / macro
# anywhere in the crate may construct a value conferring write authority over this registry,
# the gate code, or the composition root. The registry exposes zero `pub fn` taking `&mut`,
# zero `pub static mut`, zero interior-mutability handle — the only way to change it is a
# human editing the file through the normal CI suite (out-of-band-only / operator-ruling).
#   - HARD line (removal_authority=out-of-band-only): core kernel authority + circuit breaker
#     (kernel/src/breaker/, item 9) + the order/money/decide-fold core. Never editable by AI.
#     i.e. this registry + the classifier/monotonicity scripts).
# D11 Q5 (operator ruling, 2026-07-20): row REMOVAL requires a DECISIONS.md D-entry marker
#   (e.g. "D12:" referenced in the row's history / commit msg). Monotonicity guard enforces it.
# Addition of a row is always safe (widens protection) → GREEN. Removal without a D-entry
#   marker → RED (the corrigibility-adjacent attack: quietly drop a protected path).
# (1) PRODUCT RED-LINES — money / auth / capability / migrations / bulk-op.

column4: Integer-only checked money arithmetic; RED LINE: zero float on monetary values. D11 Q4: order/money core.

DenyByDefault + AllowList: runtime-plane red-line set (money/auth/secrets/MIGRATIONS/destructive-bulk). D11 Q4: core authority. NOTE: agent scope is AI-editable under token per Q4 but the SCOPE FILE sits in the core-authority boundary; Q4 marks it removal_authority=operator-ruling-required.
pgrust SQL adapter boundary (OFF-by-default feature); bulk MSRV-destructive store surface — migrations/bulk-op red-line. D11 Q4: core authority.
